content.problemStatement:
Chef recently saw the movie Matrix. He loved the movie overall but he didn't agree with some things in it. Particularly he didn't agree with the bald boy when he declared - There is no spoon. Being a chef, he understands the importance of the spoon and realizes that the universe can't survive without it. Furthermore, he is sure there is a spoon; he saw it in his kitchen this morning. So he has set out to prove the bald boy is wrong and find a spoon in the matrix. He has even obtained a digital map already. Can you help him?


Formally you're given a matrix of lowercase and uppercase Latin letters. Your job is to find out if the word "Spoon" occurs somewhere in the matrix or not. A word is said to be occurred in the matrix if it is presented in some row from left to right or in some column from top to bottom. Note that match performed has to be case insensitive.
constraints: Each matrix cell contains an English letter (a-z or A-Z)
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains two integers R and C – the number of rows and columns of the matrix. Then R lines follow, each containing exactly C alphabetic characters (uppercase or lowercase) representing the matrix.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print 'There is a spoon!' if the word 'spoon' (case-insensitive) appears consecutively in any row from left to right or in any column from top to bottom, otherwise print 'There is indeed no spoon!'.

sampleTestCases.input:
3
3 6
abDefb
bSpoon
NIKHil
6 6
aaaaaa
ssssss
xuisdP
oooooo
ioowoo
bdylan
6 5
bdfhj
cacac
opqrs
ddddd
india
yucky
sampleTestCases.output:
There is a spoon!
There is a spoon!
There is indeed no spoon!
sampleTestCases.explanation: In the first test case the word appears in the second row. In the second test case the letters 'spOon' appear vertically in the last column. The third test case contains no such sequence.
The single column spells 'spoon' from top to bottom (case-insensitive).
The letters are split across two rows; only contiguous sequences within a single row or column count.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by iterating over each row and column of the matrix and checking if the word 'spoon' (case-insensitive) appears consecutively. This can be achieved by converting each row and column to lowercase and using a string search function to find the word 'spoon'.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * R * C) per test case, O(T * R * C) total. The time complexity is linear with respect to the number of test cases, rows, and columns.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(R * C) — the dominant structure is the matrix.
